title: Megalastrum haitiense
taxonomy: Megalastrum haitiense is classified within the family Ophioglossaceae, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as adder's tongue ferns. This family is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and their allies. The classification of Megalastrum haitiense is further divided into the genus Megalastrum and the species M. haitiense. The genus Megalastrum is a group of ferns native to the Americas. Megalastrum haitiense is a species of fern that is endemic to Haiti.
uses: Megalastrum haitiense, also known as the Haitian Megalastrum, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
